perturbation.original_text: The Agent shall receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: a. The fee shall be equal to 5.2% of the "gross proceeds" received in the Offering attributable to the efforts of the Agent. At least 48 hours prior to each Closing, the Agent shall deliver a schedule, identified as Appendix A, that lists each investor and the amount of investment. 2 b. Agent shall be reimbursed for expenses as contemplated by Section 8 of this Agreement, regardless of whether the Offering is successfully completed.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agent shall receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: a. The fee shall be equal to 5.2% of the "gross proceeds" received in the Offering attributable to the efforts of the Agent, as long as there are gross proceeds. At least 48 hours prior to each Closing, the Agent shall deliver a schedule, identified as Appendix A, that lists each investor and the amount of investment. 2 b. Agent shall be reimbursed for expenses as contemplated by Section 8 of this Agreement, only if the Offering is successfully completed.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "as long as there are gross proceeds", to the definition of the fees that the agent receives, this creates a condition. By adding 'only if the Offering is successfully completed' to the definition of reimbursement of expenses, which contradicts with the section 8, this introduces an in-text contradiction about when the agent is eligible to be reimbursed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company covenants and agrees with the Agent that it will pay or cause to be paid the following: (i) the fees, disbursements and expenses of counsel to the Company and accountants in connection with the registration of the Shares under the Act and all other expenses in connection with the preparation, printing and filing of the Registration Statement, each Preliminary Prospectus, the Prospectus, each Permitted Free Writing Prospectus and any amendments or supplements thereto, and the mailing and delivering of copies thereof to the Agent and dealers; (ii) the cost of printing or reproducing this agreement, the Blue Sky Survey, any dealer agreements and any other documents in connection with the offering, purchase, sale and delivery of the Shares; (iii) all expenses in connection with the qualification of the Shares for offering and sale under state securities laws; (iv) the cost of preparing stock certificates; (v) all expenses related to road shows; (vi) the costs or expenses of any transfer agent or registrar; and (vi) all reasonable out-of-pocket fees and expenses of the Agent, including the reasonable fees and expenses of counsel for the Agent related to the Offering and not otherwise specifically provided for in this Section; the total for all such reasonable out-of-pocket fees and expenses shall not exceed $5,000 without the consent of the Company (exclusive of any blue sky-related fees if the Agent's counsel is requested to complete such services by the Company).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Agent shall be reimbursed for expenses as contemplated by Section 8, 'regardless of whether the Offering is successfully completed'. The changed text modifies this to 'only if the Offering is successfully completed'. This directly contradicts the original agreement and creates an in-text contradiction with the Contradicted Text, because the Contradicted Text describes the Company's agreement to pay various fees and expenses, which implies those expenses will be covered regardless of the success of the offering. Therefore, the change introduces a condition that directly conflicts with the pre-existing obligation outlined in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: The Company and the Agent shall transmit all funds received from subscribers to the Escrow Agent by noon of the next business day following receipt thereof. The Company shall direct the Escrow Agent to make payment for Shares sold hereunder by wire transfer or certified or bank cashier's check drawn to the order of the Company in next day funds. Such payment is to be made at the offices of Flag Bank, at 10:00 a.m. local time, on each Closing Date or at another time agreed to by the Agent and the Company. The time of such payment is referred to as the "Closing Time." The Company shall direct the Escrow Agent to deliver payment of the fees due to the Agent pursuant to Section 2 hereof (less any portion thereof previously paid to the Agent) to the Agent by wire transfer or certified or bank cashier's check drawn to the order of the Agent in next day funds, to the Agent on each Closing Date.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and the Agent shall transmit all funds received from subscribers to the Escrow Agent at their earliest convenience. The Company may direct the Escrow Agent to make payment for Shares sold hereunder by wire transfer or certified or bank cashier's check drawn to the order of the Company in next day funds. Such payment may be made at the offices of Flag Bank, at any time agreed to by the Agent and the Company. The time of such payment is referred to as the "Closing Time." The Company may direct the Escrow Agent to deliver payment of the fees due to the Agent pursuant to Section 2 hereof (less any portion thereof previously paid to the Agent) to the Agent by wire transfer or certified or bank cashier's check drawn to the order of the Agent in next day funds, whenever it deems it necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The original text required the funds received from subscribers to be transmitted to the Escrow Agent by noon of the next business day, the new text replaces this to say 'at their earliest convenience' which contradicts with the original statement. Also, the original states the time of payment, at 10 am local time, which is then changed to 'any time agreed to by the agent and company', creating an in-text contradiction. By saying 'may direct', it removes the obligation from the company, contradicting with section 2, on making sure that agent gets paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Agent shall receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: a. The fee shall be equal to 5.2% of the "gross proceeds" received in the Offering attributable to the efforts of the Agent, as long as there are gross proceeds. At least 48 hours prior to each Closing, the Agent shall deliver a schedule, identified as Appendix A, that lists each investor and the amount of investment. 2 b. Agent shall be reimbursed for expenses as contemplated by Section 8 of this Agreement, only if the Offering is successfully completed.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that funds must be transmitted to the Escrow Agent by noon of the next business day, and payment should be made at 10:00 a.m. The changed text modifies this to 'at their earliest convenience' and 'any time agreed to by the Agent and the Company,' creating a contradiction regarding the timing of the transfer and payment. The original text provides a specific deadline and time, while the changed text introduces flexibility, thereby contradicting the previous fixed time, and making the obligations inconsistent with the contradicted text (The Agent shall receive the following compensation). The removal of the obligation to pay the agent in a timely manner creates a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: All representations, warranties and agreements contained in this Agreement of the Company and the Agent shall remain in full force and effect, regardless of any termination or cancellation of this Agreement, and shall survive delivery of and payment for the Shares.
perturbation.changed_text: All representations, warranties and agreements contained in this Agreement of the Company and the Agent shall remain in full force and effect, until termination or cancellation of this Agreement, and shall not survive delivery of and payment for the Shares.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the all representations, warranties and agreements shall remain in full force, and survive delivery of and payment for the shares, which means that these agreements still apply after this has occurred. Changing this to say that all representations, warranties and agreements do not survive, creates an in-text contradiction of whether this is enforced after delivery.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company represents and warrants to the Agent that: a. The Company has all such power, authority, authorizations, approvals and orders as may be required to enter into this Agreement, to carry out the provisions and conditions hereof and to issue and sell the Shares as contemplated herein and as described in the Registration Statement, any Preliminary Prospectuses, the Prospectus or any Permitted Free Writing Prospectus. The consummation of the Offering, the execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement and the consummation of the transactions contemplated herein have been duly and validly authorized by all necessary corporate action on the part of the Company and this Agreement has been validly executed and delivered by the Company and is the valid, legal and binding agreement of the Company enforceable in accordance with its terms, except to the extent, if any, that the provisions of Section 10 hereof may be unenforceable as against public policy, and except to the extent that such enforceability may be limited by bankruptcy laws, insolvency laws, or other laws affecting the enforcement of creditors' rights generally.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the representations, warranties, and agreements survive delivery and payment. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that they do not survive delivery and payment. This creates a clear and direct contradiction. The contradicted text then relies on the company holding certain powers and authorities when entering into the agreement, which is a form of representation or warranty. If these do not survive delivery and payment for the shares, as the changed text states, then the company may not be held to these representations/warranties after the fact, creating a contradiction about the company's obligations.
